items.content: To create a new UI component in Figma, I first duplicate an existing similar component to ensure consistency in structure and properties. Then, I isolate the elements that need modification, such as icons, text labels, or color fills. Finally, I update the element's content and styling, ensuring all constraints and auto-layout settings remain correct before publishing the changes to the team's design library.

When preparing for a matcha tasting tour, I always research local cafes known for high-quality matcha and unique brewing methods. I create a flexible itinerary, noting opening hours and potential travel times between locations. Before visiting, I review the typical flavor profiles of different matcha grades to better appreciate the nuances during the tasting.

For my illustration Instagram, I schedule posts using a content calendar, aiming for a consistent upload frequency of 3-4 times a week. I batch create artwork and captions over the weekend, then use a scheduling tool to manage distribution throughout the week. Engagement is key, so I dedicate 15 minutes daily to respond to comments and interact with other artists.

To ensure smooth operation of our retro-gaming stream, I follow a pre-stream checklist: test audio levels for all participants, verify game capture is working correctly, and check internet stability. During the stream, I monitor the chat for technical issues or audience questions, and the co-host handles the game commentary. Post-stream, we save the VOD and discuss potential improvements for the next session.

When listing resin jewellery on Etsy, I optimize product titles and descriptions with relevant keywords for searchability. I take multiple high-quality photos from different angles, showcasing the piece's details and scale, often using natural light. Setting clear shipping policies and processing times is crucial for customer satisfaction and managing expectations.
